title: QUIC CRT dormant early-data attempt readiness verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests, runtime inspection, and regression evidence.
scope: Verify that the managed client/runtime path surfaces a narrow dormant early-data attempt-readiness observation only when the detached carrier still has both resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite material from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0139, that later client setup keeps the carrier dormant, that the new material stays absent when the carrier lacks the early-data prerequisite signal, that early data remains explicitly closed, and that no broad 0-RTT, anti-replay, or key-update promise appears.
preconditions: The repository builds successfully on net10.0.
REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137 and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0139 remain landed prerequisites.
The readiness observation remains internal-only and is not part of the public API.

supplemental_sections.heading: Acceptance Criteria
supplemental_sections.content:
- The dormant detached carrier reports readiness only when it still has the resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite snapshot.
- Later managed client setup keeps the carrier dormant while exposing that narrow readiness observation.
- The new material stays absent when the carrier lacks the `early_data` signal, and early data remains explicitly closed before and after capture.
- The slice does not claim 0-RTT packet support, anti-replay, key update, or any public API widening.

status_summary: This verification artifact records the internal dormant early-data attempt-readiness slice and points at the requirement-home and runtime evidence that will prove the richer dormant carrier is recognized without opening early data or widening the public promise.
expected_result: The managed client/runtime path recognizes the dormant early-data attempt-ready carrier only when the carrier has both the resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite material, and the repository does not widen its public promise.
procedure: Inspect the detached carrier, runtime readiness observation, and later client setup seam for a readiness marker that is true only when both resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite material are present.
Verify that later client setup stores the richer detached carrier as dormant internal state without opening early data.
Verify that a carrier without the `early_data` signal leaves the readiness boundary closed while the existing resumption credential carrier still works.
Verify that early data remains explicitly closed and that the accepted abbreviated resumption regression remains unchanged.
Verify that the public surface still does not expose broad 0-RTT, anti-replay, or key-update promises.

Run the focused REQ_QUIC_CRT_0139 and REQ_QUIC_CRT_0140 requirement-home tests, the current CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and repo-local SpecTrace validation plus render checks.
